symptoms.symptom_name: Persistent dry, non-productive cough for ~3 weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A dry non-productive cough is the characteristic ACE-inhibitor cough phenotype, but is non-specific in isolation.
A dry cough without sputum makes bacterial pneumonia less likely but does not exclude atypical infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cough onset within weeks of starting a new ACE-inhibitor antihypertensive

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The temporal link between commencing an ACE inhibitor and a new dry cough is the single most decisive feature for the iatrogenic diagnosis.
Upper-airway tickle reflects bradykinin/substance-P sensitisation of airway sensory nerves typical of ACE-inhibitor cough.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cough worse supine (worse on lying flat)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Supine worsening fits airway-hypersensitivity cough but is non-specific.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute pulmonary oedema / left ventricular failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Supine-worse cough can be an orthopnoea equivalent — this is precisely why the breathlessness screen is required before excluding heart failure.
Nocturnal supine reflux can trigger cough; less likely without dyspepsia.
ACE-inhibitor cough is characteristically refractory to antitussives and only resolves on drug withdrawal.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever or systemic infective symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/systemic features makes an infective cause much less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No breathlessness at rest, no orthopnoea, no paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute pulmonary oedema / left ventricular failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative breathlessness/orthopnoea/PND screen with normal oxygenation makes decompensated heart failure unlikely — the key mimic to exclude by history in telehealth.
Absence of blood is consistent with a benign iatrogenic cough and argues against malignancy/infection/PE.
Reasonably controlled home BP is relevant to the safety of ceasing/switching the antihypertensive; patient-obtainable and admissible in telehealth, unverified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute pulmonary oedema / left ventricular failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal home oxygenation makes significant pulmonary oedema/consolidation less likely; a home-device reading, so unverified and not a substitute for examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear chest on auscultation — no wheeze or crackles, good bilateral air entry

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute pulmonary oedema / left ventricular failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bibasal crackles argues strongly against pulmonary oedema — but this is a sealed clinician auscultation the AI Doctor cannot obtain; it must infer likelihood from history, not a measured exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cardiovascular exam — dual heart sounds, no murmurs, no peripheral oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute pulmonary oedema / left ventricular failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No peripheral oedema or added heart sounds argues against heart failure — sealed clinician examination, not remotely obtainable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild posterior pharyngeal erythema, no exudate (mechanical irritation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical pharyngeal irritation from repeated coughing; the absence of exudate argues against bacterial pharyngitis. Sealed clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest radiograph (planned) — expected normal / no consolidation or oedema

investigation_details.expected_result: No consolidation, no pulmonary oedema — supporting a benign iatrogenic cough
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Not required for a classic ACE-inhibitor cough with a negative red-flag screen; reserved for persistent or red-flag cases.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging would exclude consolidation/oedema if the diagnosis were in doubt; a sealed investigation not available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A dry, antitussive-resistant, throat-tickle cough beginning within weeks of an ACE inhibitor is a highly characteristic pattern that points to the iatrogenic cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Medication-timing history (link cough onset to ACE-inhibitor start)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The decisive, zero-cost diagnostic step — establishes the iatrogenic cause via history alone.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic metabolic panel / renal function and electrolytes (on switching to an ARB)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Appropriate before/after changing the antihypertensive class; performed by the GP, not urgent.
Not indicated for a classic ACE-inhibitor cough with a negative red-flag screen; over-ordering is low-value. Reserve for persistent, atypical or red-flag presentations.
